svg.text: An OpenClaw AI agent started everything. Known AppSec practices could have stopped it at every step.
February 20 - March 24, 2026 | 5 ecosystems | 1,000+ compromised SaaS environments | 33 days | CVE-2026-33634 (CVSS 9.4)
WHAT WOULD HAVE STOPPED THIS (KNOWN APPSEC PRACTICES, NOT IMPLEMENTED)

svg.text: DEVELOPER AWARENESS + COMPROMISED ENVIRONMENTS OVER TIME
Security researchers warned for 33 days. The AI community listened to one person.
"Software horror: litellm PyPI supply chain attack"
NOW the AI developer community pays attention.
33 days of advisories from Wiz, Socket, StepSecurity, Aikido, Sysdig, CrowdStrike, Microsoft. CVE assigned (CVSS 9.4). Mandiant confirms 1,000+ compromised environments.
None of it registered with the broader AI developer community until one AI thought leader reframed it. The practices that would have prevented 70-100% of this chain have existed for years.
Data: Wiz, Socket, Aikido, Sysdig, Endor Labs, StepSecurity, Snyk, BleepingComputer, Mandiant, Microsoft | March 24, 2026
